run_summary: Portfolio up +$77 in two days on NTLA gene-editing tailwind and ALK airline fuel relief. Both theses remain intact: NTLA's 8-K catalyst is confirmed (UniQure FDA news + XBI +3%), ALK's Iran deal / oil downside frame holds. Today's flag pool includes three gene-editing re-ratings (NTLA re-entry, PTCT, BEAM), multiple defense names correcting off overshooting geopolitical moves (AVAV re-entry warning, KTOS, MRCY), and sector sympathy fades (VECO semi, AMR energy, AYI industrial). MASI re-entry warning is a closed-thesis name with no forward catalyst. Concentration in Healthcare (NTLA, PTCT, BEAM) will breach the 40% sector cap if all three are entered; prioritizing highest-conviction, smallest exploratory bets to preserve flexibility.

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: PTCT 8-K (8.01) filed 6/16 plus broader biotech bid
entries.thesis: PTCT up sharply on roughly 2x volume with an 8-K filed yesterday (items 8.01+9.01, i.e., 'other event' with exhibits — typically a press release for a regulatory update, partnership, or pipeline news). XBI +3% provides a supportive tape. Move appears appropriate to a non-trivial disclosure rather than excessive; without reading the exhibit I can't argue it's underdone, but the combination of own filing + group strength makes the magnitude defensible.
entries.what_kills: Exhibit is routine corporate update with no new value-driving content — would suggest the move overshot and is faded once read
entries.reasoning: PTCT 8-K (items 8.01 + 9.01) filed 6/16 on roughly 2x volume (+7.18% move, +1.98x volume multiple) into strong XBI tape (+3%). The move is moderate and proportional—not an overreaction—which suggests the exhibit contains a real catalyst (regulatory action, partnership, or pipeline milestone) rather than a trivial disclosure. The thesis is coherent: biotech 8-K exhibit signals non-routine news into supportive sector tape. Confidence 3 (RATIONAL not OVERDONE/UNDERDONE) and move is justified, so this is an exploratory tier candidate (6% of equity). Will learn from the exhibit content whether the move was justified or if a fade is coming. Current portfolio healthcare exposure is ~32% (NTLA); adding PTCT at 6% (~$600) brings it to ~38%, safely under 40% sector cap.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case explicitly acknowledges it has not read the 8-K exhibit and cannot verify whether the catalyst is meaningful or routine, making the entire thesis dependent on an unvalidated assumption.
red_team.critique: Haiku's reasoning rests on inferring catalyst substance from the *magnitude* of the move (7.18% on 2x volume) and sector support (XBI +3%), but this is circular logic: the move itself cannot prove the exhibit is non-trivial. The five-day change of 7.06% shows the stock has already moved in line with the single-day spike, suggesting the market has already priced the news. Without reading the exhibit, there is no way to distinguish between a genuine regulatory milestone and a routine corporate disclosure that the market temporarily overweighted. The exploratory tier (6% position) is appropriate risk management, but the thesis remains purely speculative on catalyst quality.

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: UniQure announcing FDA filing for Huntington's gene therapy lifting gene-editing group; NTLA Reg FD 8-K on 6/15
entries.thesis: NTLA up modestly today but a notable multi-day run (extended five-day gain) in sympathy with UniQure's announcement that it will seek FDA approval for its Huntington's gene therapy. Combined with NTLA's own 6/15 8-K (Reg FD + 8.01), this looks like a coherent, justified re-rating of gene-edit/gene-therapy regulatory optionality rather than an overreaction. Risk-off tape did not prevent the move — XBI +3% confirms group bid.
entries.what_kills: NTLA 8-K turns out to be a routine conference presentation deck with no new data; UniQure FDA process stalls or pushback from agency reappears; XBI rolls over with broader risk-off resumption.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning applies: NTLA was already entered 1 day ago at $12.79 and is now +18.71% unrealized. The new flag today re-describes the same UniQure catalyst and XBI tailwind, but this is NOT new information—it's the same tape effect and the same 8-K we are already holding. The flag confidence is only 3 (RATIONAL, not OVERDONE or UNDERDONE), and the re-entry discipline requires confidence ≥4 for a re-entry on genuinely new information. The information here is already known and priced into the position we hold. No new information since entry 24 hours ago. WATCH only.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with UniQure FDA filing announcement
entries.thesis: BEAM up alongside NTLA on a strong XBI tape with no company-specific 8-K. The UniQure Huntington's FDA pathway announcement is a regulatory sentiment positive for the entire base-editing/in-vivo editing cohort. Magnitude appears proportional to a group re-rating rather than excessive.
entries.what_kills: XBI rolls over with no follow-through; UniQure FDA process hits a snag; tape risk-off resumes broadly
entries.reasoning: BEAM is rallying in sympathy with UniQure FDA news + XBI strength (up 4.81% on 1.09x volume, 5-day +10.14%). No company-specific catalyst—pure group re-rating. The thesis (regulatory thaw for gene-editing cohort) is identical to NTLA's thesis, which we already own. Adding BEAM would represent a doubling-down on the same directional bet (gene-editing group tailwind) without incremental differentiation. Healthcare would breach 40% if both PTCT and BEAM are entered alongside NTLA. BEAM can be revisited if it consolidates or if a BEAM-specific 8-K catalyst surfaces (e.g., partnership or trial data). For now, WATCH and preserve sector flexibility for higher-conviction setups.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; sympathy with SMH -4.8% and broader semi sell-off (AVGO, TSM, NVDA all down hard)
entries.thesis: VECO ran up notably over the past week alongside the semi-equipment cohort (ONTO, AEIS up similar amounts) and is now unwinding harder than the SMH index because of low float and momentum positioning. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this is sector beta plus crowding unwind, and an outsized one-day drop in a name that just rallied tends to overshoot fundamentals.
entries.what_kills: If a delayed news source surfaces a VECO-specific negative (customer push-out, downgrade with channel checks), the move is fundamentally driven and not a fade.
entries.reasoning: VECO is flagged OVERDONE confidence 3 (down -9.44% today, +11.92% five-day, 1.32x volume on the drop). The thesis is correct: semi-equipment small-cap rallied on group momentum (ONTO, AEIS up ~12-15% in 5 days) and is now unwinding on a broad SMH -4.8% selloff with no company-specific catalyst. This is a crowded-trade reversion setup, not a fundamental deterioration. However, the horizon is 'days' and the move has already given back 9.44% in a single session. A fade candidate, but entry here means buying after the sharp intraday move has already materialized. WATCH for stabilization of SMH + a second day of relative weakness before entering on a more defined chart setup. Confidence in the OVERDONE thesis is 4, but timing is poor.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with XLE / oil weakness on US-Iran deal framework
entries.thesis: AMR -5.24% today and -5.89% over 5 days as XLE -3.48% on the US-Iran deal — but AMR is coking coal for steel, not thermal coal or oil. The fundamental linkage to Iran/Hormuz oil supply is weak; met coal pricing tracks Chinese steel demand and Australian export tonnages, not Brent. Below-average volume (0.77x) on the selloff suggests passive ETF/energy-complex outflows rather than dedicated fundamental selling. Setup for a relative-value bounce if energy stabilizes.
entries.what_kills: Hard coking coal benchmark prices roll over; Chinese steel demand prints weak; AMR-specific operational issue surfaces
entries.reasoning: AMR flagged OVERDONE confidence 3, down -5.24% / -5.89% 5-day on energy complex sympathy despite weak fundamental linkage (coking coal ≠ thermal / oil). Below-1x volume (0.77x) on the selloff is indeed a tell of ETF mechanics, not dedicated selling. However, the thesis horizon is 'weeks' and the thesis itself requires a catalyst (oil/XLE stabilization) to work. Current portfolio is already long directional beta (ALK benefits from oil down, would hurt from oil up). Adding a coking coal name as a hedge would create cross-correlation issues. WATCH for a cleaner entry when XLE has stabilized for 2+ days and coking coal benchmarks firm. Confidence in the OVERDONE thesis is 4, but portfolio hedging doesn't favor this entry now.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Iran deal-signing headline + broader defense weakness (ITA -0.95% on risk-on day)
entries.thesis: AVAV's drone-heavy revenue mix is disproportionately exposed to active conflict demand. With Trump admin signaling Iran deal signing 'in coming days' and ITA underperforming, the de-rating could extend if a deal actually prints. No 8-K signal so this is sector/macro driven, not company-specific — but the divergence vs. a risk-on tape is notable.
entries.what_kills: Iran deal falls through (Trump-admin source said 'not 100% certain'); AVAV announces a new DoD contract; broader defense bounces as buyers treat dip as opportunity
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning applies: AVAV was closed 15 days ago at a -5.31% realized loss on OVERDONE confidence 3 (Iran missile headline + defense sector bid). The new flag today re-flags AVAV as UNDERDONE confidence 3 on identical catalyst (Iran deal framing, defense sector weakness). However, the re-entry discipline requires confidence ≥4 and genuinely NEW information. The information here is NOT new: the flag still cites Iran de-escalation and the defense sector move. The only difference is the classification flipped from OVERDONE to UNDERDONE, but this is an interpretive shift, not new data. Exit reasoning 15 days ago was correct (geopolitical premium was overextended); the new flag is the same tape at a lower price, which does not constitute new information sufficient to overcome the re-entry bar. WATCH and require either a BEAM/AVAV-specific contract award OR confirmation that the Iran deal has materially stalled before re-entering.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.catalyst: Defense sympathy on Iran headlines + IWM strength
entries.thesis: KTOS up 7% but five_day_change is -7.3% — this looks like a bounce off a defense pullback rather than fresh strength. The puzzling part: if Iran is settling, the geopolitical premium that drove defense names higher should unwind, not extend. ITA +5% suggests the market is reading 'Trump shows force → deals get done → more defense spend, not less,' but that read may not hold once the deal language firms up. KTOS is high-beta defense; the move likely overshoots and fades when the de-escalation reality sinks in.
entries.what_kills: Deal falls through and conflict escalates, in which case KTOS extends. Or DoD budget news / specific Kratos contract award surfaces that justifies the move on fundamentals not geopolitics.
entries.reasoning: KTOS flagged OVERDONE confidence 3: up +7.22% today but five_day_change is -7.29%, which indicates a bounce off a decline rather than genuine strength. The flagged thesis is correct—this is a geopolitical premium unwind as Iran de-escalates. However, the move has already happened (bounce is already in the price) and the five-day context shows the name is still negative. Entering on the bounce of a five-day decline is chasing mean-reversion without a clear catalyst to confirm the direction. No 8-K or contract news to anchor conviction. Confidence in the OVERDONE thesis is 4 (correct characterization), but the entry point is poor. SKIP for now; would revisit as a short/fade candidate if ITA re-accelerates and KTOS continues higher without contract news.

entries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
entries.catalyst: Defense sympathy + risk-on tape; no company-specific catalyst
entries.thesis: MRCY up nearly 12% on 1.25x volume with no SEC filing and no earnings catalyst — yet 5-day change is barely positive. That pattern says the move is concentrated entirely in today's session and likely driven by a short squeeze or sympathy with ITA strength, not fundamentals. Defense small-caps with high short interest often spike sharply on risk-on days then fade. Without a confirming catalyst this looks like a momentum/squeeze move that overshoots.
entries.what_kills: A Mercury-specific contract win or analyst upgrade surfaces; or short interest data shows the move was structurally supported by genuine new buyers, not squeeze mechanics.
entries.reasoning: MRCY flagged OVERDONE confidence 3: up +11.71% today on 1.25x volume, but five_day_change is only +1.27%, confirming that the entire move is concentrated in this one session with no broad tape support. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst. The flagged thesis (short squeeze / momentum spike) is correct. However, the setup is not actionable: entering after an 11.71% gap-up move on what is likely a squeeze means buying into the momentum that should fade, not timing the fade. Better to let the squeeze fully reverse over 1-3 days and revisit if a contrarian entry signals material support. SKIP for now; would consider a short/fade if ITA stabilizes and MRCY closes unchanged or lower tomorrow.

entries.name: Masimo Corporation
entries.catalyst: Multi-item 8-K consistent with closing of a previously-telegraphed divestiture or spin-off
entries.thesis: The 8-K bundles Item 1.02 (terminated agreement), 2.01 (completed disposition), 3.01 (delisting/listing rule), 3.03 (security holder rights modification), 5.01 (change in control), 5.02 (officer/director change), and 5.03 (charter amendment). That combination is the fingerprint of a closed spin-off or divestiture of the consumer/Sound United business — long anticipated by activists. Stock flat on triple volume suggests the market views the transaction terms as in-line with what was already priced in. Magnitude (zero move) is rational given the event was telegraphed.
entries.what_kills: If the 8-K reveals an unexpected dilutive financing, a surprise CEO/leadership shake-up, or terms materially worse than expected, the flat tape becomes the mispricing and downside opens up.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning applies: MASI was closed 1 day ago at a -0.22% realized loss. The new flag today re-describes the same 8-K (Sound United divestiture closure), and the setup is identical: stock flat on 3x volume, market pricing the deal in-line with prior expectations. Exit reasoning 1 day ago was correct (thesis fully resolved, no forward catalyst). The flag's confidence is 4 (RATIONAL, conviction-level), but the information is NOT new—it's the same transaction closure event we already exited. The re-entry discipline requires confidence ≥4 AND new information. While confidence is met, there is zero new information here beyond the 8-K we already saw. The thesis is PLAYED-OUT per the original exit. WATCH only and avoid re-chasing a closed event. Would only re-enter if a major activist commentary or a surprise spin-off of a divested unit were announced within 48 hours.

entries.name: Acuity Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with industrials sector (XLI -3.4%) on hot CPI + Iran headlines, no company-specific news
entries.thesis: XLI -3.4% today, AYI -6.5% with no 8-K and volume only 1.07x average. That's roughly 2x sector beta on an industrial that's now down ~10% over five days. The lack of any volume confirmation or filing suggests pure sympathy/multiple compression from the CPI-driven rate-up move, not company-specific deterioration. Lighting/electrical demand is cyclical but Acuity's quality and balance sheet argue against the magnitude.
entries.what_kills: An after-hours 8-K appearing tonight or tomorrow (especially 2.05 restructuring or guidance withdrawal); a competitor pre-announcement on commercial construction weakness.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning applies: AYI was closed 0 days ago (today) at a +2.77% realized gain. The new flag today re-flags AYI with identical thesis (OVERDONE sympathy with industrials, XLI down, no 8-K, weeks horizon). Exit reasoning today was that the original OVERDONE conviction broke because the name continued DOWN another -6.5% (5-day -10.37%) instead of fading as the thesis predicted. The new flag re-describes the same decline as present-day action. Re-entry on the same thesis (sympathy fade) after the name has already moved against the thesis makes no sense—the thesis is broken, not reset. The re-entry discipline requires confidence ≥4 AND new information. The flag confidence is 3 (RATIONAL, not conviction), which automatically downgrades to WATCH per the rules. Additionally, there is NO new information—the flag repeats the same 'XLI down, no 8-K' setup that just proved false. WATCH and avoid re-buying a thesis that failed in real time.

entries.name: Saia, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possible LTL tonnage data, downgrade, or peer preannouncement
entries.thesis: Sharp multi-day decline on no visible catalyst — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings. XLI is roughly flat (-0.14%), so this is not sector tape. Could be a monthly LTL tonnage update, competitor preannouncement, or analyst downgrade not captured in the news feed. Cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the driver.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings catalyst visible
entries.thesis: Multi-day decline in a software name with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings. XLK is only down 0.34% so this is not pure tech tape. Could be sell-side derating on supply-chain software demand concerns, a competitor read-through, or a slow-bleed sentiment shift; without an identifiable catalyst I cannot commit to overdone/underdone at conf 3+.

entries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
entries.catalyst: Sector risk-off in consumer discretionary on hawkish Fed re-statement
entries.thesis: DECK down on below-average volume on a day XLY is -2.5% and the broader risk-off impulse from a more hawkish Fed is hitting discretionary hardest. No company-specific catalyst, no 8-K, no earnings. Magnitude is roughly consistent with sector beta plus mid-cap discretionary skew — nothing to fade or chase.
entries.what_kills: DECK accelerates lower on rising volume tomorrow without sector follow-through, suggesting an undisclosed name-specific issue

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with semi-cap weakness; possible minor digestion of June 12 8-K
entries.thesis: Same pattern as VECO/ONTO — AEIS rallied substantially over 5 days, today gives back on broad semi weakness. The June 12 8-K is items 8.01/9.01 only (catch-all + exhibits), which is almost never severely material on its own. Below-average volume on the down move suggests profit-taking, not panic.
entries.what_kills: If the 8.01 filing contained a soft pre-announcement or guidance reset language, the move is justified and not a fade.

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with semi/Apple-supplier weakness; possible digestion of June 12 material agreement
entries.thesis: QRVO is moving roughly in line with the broader semi/Apple-supply complex on a clear sector-down day, but the June 12 1.01 filing (material definitive agreement) is non-trivial — could be M&A, a big customer contract, a debt covenant amendment, or a divestiture. Without reading the exhibit, the direction the 1.01 should push the stock is unknowable, and today's move is consistent with pure semi-tape sympathy regardless.
entries.what_kills: Without the exhibit, neither side rises above conf 3.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semicap sector strength tied to AI/HBM packaging narrative
entries.thesis: ONTO has run ~28% in 5 days alongside VECO and AEIS. Unlike VECO, ONTO has direct advanced packaging/inspection exposure tied to AI-driven HBM demand, which has been a documented bullish theme. Today's move is consistent with sector beta on a strong SMH day. No 8-K, no obvious mispricing direction.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Risk-on tape + housing/builder bid; older 5.02 filing context
entries.thesis: BLDR has a recent 5.02 (officer/director departure or appointment) filing from 2026-06-05 — six days before today's move. The move is occurring on below-average volume (0.69x) into a strong risk-on tape with rate-sensitive names bid. The 5.02 by itself doesn't tell us whether the change was bullish (new CEO, market liked it) or bearish (CFO departure, would weigh on stock). Hard to commit a direction without reading the filing text.
entries.what_kills: Today's move is purely beta to IWM/housing tape and the 5.02 is irrelevant noise.

entries.name: Insmed Incorporated
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no SEC filing or news in our feed
entries.thesis: No filings, no news, modest 1.3x volume on a -7% day with XBI -1.9%. Move is roughly 4x the sector beta but I can't articulate a specific bull or bear thesis without knowing the cause. Could be brensocatib commercial concern, a clinical leak, or a single fund unwind.
entries.what_kills: If a clean explanation appears (broker conference comment, secondary offering rumor), the call should be re-evaluated immediately.

entries.name: WEX Inc.
entries.thesis: WEX -7.4% on essentially average volume (1.02x), no 8-K, no news. WEX has dual exposure to fleet fuel cards (oil sensitivity, but generally positive when prices rise via spread) and corporate travel payments. Direction of oil sensitivity is ambiguous — historically WEX has been a beneficiary of higher fuel prices, not a victim. Can't construct a clean conf-3 thesis either direction.
entries.what_kills: Tape stabilizes and stock recovers — suggests illiquid mid-cap algo overshoot.
